scope: Sensitivity of the predeclared 106.0 m astrodynamics-grounded Cramer-Rao floor (release/predeclarations/astrodynamics_floor_loop47.json) to its documented approximations, including tighter floors from relaxed conservative simplifications and a looser pass-correlated effective-count floor, evaluated against the long-arc higher-fidelity force-and-density-mismatch held-out test (preferring the n=64 replication artifact results/long_arc_hifi_force_mismatch/long_arc_hifi_force_mismatch_n64_loop57.json). Each variant changes one or more documented assumptions; the predeclared pass/fail decision is re-evaluated under the variant floor without retuning the rule.

A_predeclared_baseline.description: Predeclared baseline (matches astrodynamics_floor_loop47.json): worst-case slant range at the minimum-elevation cap; no range-rate credit; no-overlap union bound.

B_mean_visible_elevation.description: Replace the worst-case (minimum-elevation) slant range with the slant range at the mean visible elevation; otherwise identical to the predeclared baseline.

C_range_rate_credit.description: Credit the range-rate channel toward the per-update position CRLB via an along-track position-equivalent sigma equal to the range-rate noise integrated over one propagator step (0.08 m/s x 20 s = 1.60 m); otherwise identical to the predeclared baseline.

D_multi_visibility_credit.description: Replace the no-overlap union bound on the network visibility fraction by a conservative multi-visibility credit factor of 1.25; otherwise identical to the predeclared baseline.

E_all_three_combined.description: Apply all three relaxations simultaneously: mean visible elevation slant range, range-rate credit, and multi-visibility credit factor 1.25. This is the simultaneously-tightest floor any single referee would reach by relaxing only the disclosed assumptions.

F_pass_correlated_effective_count.description: Pass-correlated effective-count bound: keep the predeclared slant range, no range-rate credit, and no-overlap union visibility count, but divide the visible-step count by a 10-step within-pass correlation block (200 s at the 20 s integration step). This is a conservative rough bound for temporally correlated line-of-sight residuals within a pass.

long_arc_pass_fail_audit.comment: Under this variant the DSA-EKF still does not satisfy the predeclared positive criterion because DSA-EKF is not strictly lowest in mean (AUKF mean is lowest at 312.7 m). The variant therefore does not flip the predeclared pass/fail decision.

summary.note: Across all six variants the predeclared positive criterion on the long-arc held-out test remains unsatisfied because DSA-EKF is not strictly lowest in mean. The floor sensitivity is informational and does not load-bear the predeclared decision.
